title: Don Geiss, America And Hope

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm supposed to be drinking a soda right now.
jokes.setup: Jack thanks employees for taking time from busy schedules
jokes.punchline: Employee's only obligation is drinking soda
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of corporate importance with absurd specificity about soda schedule

jokes.text: I don't know why you're wearing girls' pajamas, But I'm sure it's cultural.
jokes.setup: Jack addresses Tokyo office via simulcast
jokes.punchline: Misidentifies traditional Japanese clothing as girls' pajamas
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack cultural obliviousness delivered with confident authority

jokes.text: Not Seinfeld, Friends, E.R. exciting. More like 3d episodes of Merlin exciting.
jokes.setup: Jack says this is an exciting time for NBC
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflates with comparison to failed 3D Merlin episodes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation structure - builds up NBC's past hits only to crash into absurdly specific failure

jokes.text: Even if it is from... Philadelphia.
jokes.setup: Jack praises Kabletown as a great company
jokes.punchline: Dramatic pause before revealing his Boston prejudice against Philly
jokes.explanation: Sets up Boston vs. Philadelphia rivalry that will escalate throughout scene

jokes.text: How did you even get a snowball?
jokes.setup: Philadelphia employee throws snowball at Jack
jokes.punchline: Jack's practical concern about snowball logistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack response - missing the aggression, focusing on the practical impossibility

jokes.text: Yeah, then he looked around, realized it sucked, And moved to Philadelphia!
jokes.setup: Jack claims Benjamin Franklin as Boston's
jokes.punchline: Liz turns it around - Franklin fled Boston for Philadelphia
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of city rivalry with historical fact weaponized as insult

jokes.text: Oh, did you just whip a battery at me?
jokes.setup: City rivalry escalating to violence
jokes.punchline: Jack's incredulous reaction to being hit with a battery
jokes.explanation: References Philadelphia sports fans' reputation for throwing batteries, but less sharp than surrounding jokes

jokes.text: And the earth into one of the top three planets In the universe.
jokes.setup: Jack praises Don Geiss for building GE into greatest company on earth
jokes.punchline: Extends hyperbole to ranking Earth as only top three planet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - takes corporate hyperbole to cosmic scale while suggesting Earth isn't even #1

jokes.text: That's the craziest thing I ever heard. Episcopal!
jokes.setup: Jack mentions 'episcopal cryogenic freezing service'
jokes.punchline: Employee shocked by denomination choice, not the cryogenic freezing
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - audience expects shock at cryogenic freezing, gets religious denomination concern instead

jokes.text: This is for you, Dan Goose.
jokes.setup: Employee honors fallen leader Don Geiss with song
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces name as 'Dan Goose'
jokes.explanation: Simple name mispronunciation joke that works for character incompetence but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Well, I would have rather watched that terrible movie Five times than have hung out with Wesley.
jokes.setup: Liz compares date to romantic scene in Notting Hill
jokes.punchline: Would prefer watching terrible movie five times to the date
jokes.explanation: Good structure with Liz's characteristic self-deprecation about her dating life

jokes.text: One for the 8:00 hot tub time machine.
jokes.setup: Liz claims to buy ticket for Truffaut retrospective at art house
jokes.punchline: Actually buying ticket for lowbrow comedy Hot Tub Time Machine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - pretentious setup reveals her lowbrow reality

jokes.text: I had a thing where I kept running in to michael douglas. But then I realized it was just some old lady Who lives in my building.
jokes.setup: Liz relates to Wesley's universe theory with her Michael Douglas story
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was actually just an old lady neighbor
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic with perfect escalation - builds celebrity encounter story then deflates with mundane reality

jokes.text: No, the premier talent agency For black dwarves.
jokes.setup: Liz asks if Little Brown is the publishing house
jokes.punchline: Tracy reveals it's actually a talent agency for black dwarfs
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy non-sequitur that subverts expectations about publishing industry

jokes.text: My addiction to prescription glasses.
jokes.setup: Tracy lists embarrassing revelations from nanny's book
jokes.punchline: Addiction to prescription glasses (instead of prescription drugs)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay subverting celebrity drug addiction trope with innocent glasses

jokes.text: Jack, your shoes are shiny.
jokes.setup: Tracy mentions his attention deficit disorder
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets distracted mid-sentence by Jack's shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Tracy literally demonstrates ADD by getting distracted mid-explanation

jokes.text: Yeah. Seltzer and Mike and ikes.
jokes.setup: Wesley defending his movie snack choices
jokes.punchline: Brings bizarre combination of seltzer and candy
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Wesley's odd choices. Seltzer at movies is appropriately weird.

jokes.text: Your ben & jerry's flavor is called adulteraisin.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Tracy's supposed infidelity
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'adultery' and 'raisin' for ice cream flavor
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining adultery theme with Ben & Jerry's naming convention

jokes.text: madder Than a bat in a suitcase.
jokes.setup: Tracy describes how mad Angie will be
jokes.punchline: Uses bizarre simile about bat in suitcase
jokes.explanation: Tracy's characteristic weird metaphor, but not as sharp as his best material

jokes.text: Not the time travel. It's the hot tub. You don't just turn one on, and it's immediately hot. I should know. I've been in a hot tub two times.
jokes.setup: Wesley has problem with Hot Tub Time Machine science
jokes.punchline: Pedantic concern about hot tub heating time, with oddly specific experience count
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wesley character beat - missing the point, being pedantic, with weirdly specific detail

jokes.text: It's like being in a cathy cartoon That just won't end.
jokes.setup: Wesley criticizes Liz's food obsession
jokes.punchline: Compares her to dated Cathy comic strip
jokes.explanation: Devastating insult that perfectly captures both Liz's food obsession and Wesley's pretentious references

jokes.text: So why are you in my phone as future husband, and why am I in yours as future wife?
jokes.setup: Liz and Wesley claim they're not meant to be together
jokes.punchline: Both reveal they have each other saved as future spouse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy revealing both characters' desperation beneath their denials

jokes.text: Feel like I'm back at school learning about the dangers of book readin.
jokes.setup: Kenneth comments on all the books in Jack's office
jokes.punchline: References learning about dangers of reading (not benefits)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment revealing his backwards rural education

jokes.text: and bad things to Chinese Rivers.
jokes.setup: Jack praises GE's motto 'We bring good things to life'
jokes.punchline: Immediately acknowledges environmental damage in China
jokes.explanation: Sharp corporate satire with perfect economy - deflates corporate slogan with environmental reality

jokes.text: Second only to the company of great friends.
jokes.setup: Jack continues praising GE
jokes.punchline: Earnest, sentimental comparison to friendship
jokes.explanation: Character comedy from Jack's rare moment of sentiment, but not particularly sharp

jokes.text: My cousin in Atlanta is a business model. She holds up staplers in catalogues.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions different business models
jokes.punchline: Kenneth misunderstands, thinks of literal modeling with staplers
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth misunderstanding with perfect specificity about catalog modeling

jokes.text: In England, we call it a film pod.
jokes.setup: Wesley mentions bringing video camera
jokes.punchline: Claims British term 'film pod' for video camera
jokes.explanation: Wesley's pretentious tendency to make up British terms, but relatively mild

jokes.text: My brother is a cardiologist, and I'm just as good as he is. We drive the same car.
jokes.setup: Dr. Kaplan defends his medical credentials
jokes.punchline: Uses car ownership as evidence of equal medical skill
jokes.explanation: Good character insecurity with absurd logic about professional equality

jokes.text: We are gonna hang out so much after this. I want to take you to the big apple circus 'cause we're best friends.
jokes.setup: Liz and Wesley under anesthesia in recovery room
jokes.punchline: Liz makes overly enthusiastic friendship declarations
jokes.explanation: Standard anesthesia humor showing drug-induced oversharing and false intimacy

jokes.text: How come so many of his patients have died, But I've only killed one guy, huh?
jokes.setup: Dr. Kaplan continues comparing himself to cardiologist brother
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of killing one patient while defending his record
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - character accidentally reveals malpractice while trying to defend competence

jokes.text: I got a lot of flack After I ate the pig that played babe.
jokes.setup: Jenna offers Tracy advice about scandal management
jokes.punchline: Reveals she ate the pig from the movie Babe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - casually mentions horrifically narcissistic act with no self-awareness

jokes.text: But it's like a black barbie doll in Arizona. Nobody's buying it.
jokes.setup: Tracy says his denials aren't working
jokes.punchline: Comparison to black Barbie doll sales in Arizona
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective metaphor about credibility using racial demographics

jokes.text: Just like my favorite golfer OJ Simpson.
jokes.setup: Frank mentions black superstars taken down by personal life
jokes.punchline: Calls OJ Simpson his favorite golfer (not mentioning murder trial)
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection focusing on OJ's minor golf career while ignoring the obvious reference

jokes.text: Jack 'the master baiter' Donaghy.
jokes.setup: Dave praises Jack as his mentor in business tactics
jokes.punchline: Nickname sounds like 'masturbator' when spoken
jokes.explanation: Standard juvenile wordplay that works for character's obliviousness but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: Based on the novel 'tush' by assfire.
jokes.setup: Dave shows Jack pay-per-view porn titles
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Avatar' credit 'based on novel Push by Sapphire'
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of Avatar credits with perfect porn title formatting

jokes.text: I'm the reason the microwave tray rotates.
jokes.setup: Jack defends his Harvard credentials and innovation skills
jokes.punchline: Claims credit for mundane microwave feature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - taking credit for small innovation while defending his relevance

jokes.text: 'and Alexander wept For there were no more worlds to conquer.' Hans Gruber, Die Hard.
jokes.setup: Jack quotes philosophical line about Alexander the Great
jokes.punchline: Attributes it to villain Hans Gruber from Die Hard
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack character moment - misattributing classical quote to action movie while feeling defeated

jokes.text: Gangway for footcycle!
jokes.setup: Wesley riding bicycle and calling for people to move
jokes.punchline: Made-up pretentious term 'footcycle' for bicycle
jokes.explanation: Good Wesley character beat with his tendency toward pretentious terminology

jokes.text: Yeah, fine, my velocipede
jokes.setup: Liz objects to 'footcycle' term
jokes.punchline: Wesley switches to even more pretentious archaic term 'velocipede'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Wesley's pretentiousness with historically accurate but archaic bicycle term

jokes.text: You wouldn't be complaining If you wore the shoes I bought you. You're an old bitch.
jokes.setup: Wesley pointing to couple as example of love
jokes.punchline: They immediately demonstrate toxic relationship
jokes.explanation: Good comedic timing undermining Wesley's point. The immediate escalation to 'old bitch' is effective.

jokes.text: We're each other's settling soul mates.
jokes.setup: Wesley explains why universe keeps bringing them together
jokes.punchline: Concept of 'settling soul mates' as romantic compromise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept that captures modern dating pessimism with oxymoronic phrase

jokes.text: And I've played Monopoly alone.
jokes.setup: Liz says 'settling soul mates' is grim
jokes.punchline: Provides evidence of her own grimness with solo Monopoly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz self-awareness with devastating economy - solo Monopoly as ultimate loneliness indicator

jokes.text: You're an old bitch.
jokes.setup: Wesley asks Liz about couples who don't annoy each other
jokes.punchline: Example couple immediately demonstrates mutual annoyance
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing to prove Wesley's point about couples, though the insult itself is basic

jokes.text: It's a Liz-aster.
jokes.setup: Liz describes her relationship emergency to Jack
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'Liz' and 'disaster'
jokes.explanation: Standard character-name pun that fits Liz's tendency toward wordplay but lacks cleverness

jokes.text: 'CEO of G.E. dies violently in a casino orgy.'
jokes.setup: Jack describing what his obituary was going to say
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions extremely inappropriate death scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor showing Jack's wild past. The specific combination of violence, gambling, and sex is memorably excessive.

jokes.text: 'CEO of G.E. dies violently in a casino orgy.'
jokes.setup: Jack reflects on how his obituary has changed
jokes.punchline: His former life path would have ended in casino orgy death
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment revealing Jack's self-image and corporate excess with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: 'middle manager of a Philadelphia Pornography distributor never wakes up'?
jokes.setup: Jack contrasts his glorious former obituary path
jokes.punchline: New boring obituary as middle manager who dies in sleep
jokes.explanation: Good contrast structure showing Jack's fall from corporate glory to mundane middle management

jokes.text: A perpetual motion machine endlessly satisfying Every human need.
jokes.setup: Jack describing Kabletown's business model
jokes.punchline: Describes pornography empire in scientific/utopian terms
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about human nature and capitalism. The grandiose language contrasts well with the sordid reality.

jokes.text: I'm going to bury Don Geiss, America, and hope.
jokes.setup: Jack asks where he's going after existential crisis
jokes.punchline: Dramatically announces burial of abstract concepts along with Don Geiss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack dramatic moment - escalating from literal burial to abstract concepts with typical grandiosity

jokes.text: Hey, baby, it's your husband. So I'm at Bed Bath & Beyond, And I can't remember, Did you say to get metal or wood shower curtain rings?
jokes.setup: Leaked voicemail supposedly reveals Tracy's boring domestic life
jokes.punchline: Mundane question about shower curtain rings to wife
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Tracy's wild image and actual domestic reality with specific mundane detail

jokes.text: Reason one: I am conscious.
jokes.setup: Liz lists reasons she wouldn't sleep with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Being conscious as first disqualifier
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz deadpan with brutal economy - consciousness as basic requirement Tracy fails to meet

jokes.text: You know what I have? A sims family that keeps getting murdered.
jokes.setup: Liz contrasts her life with Tracy's stable marriage
jokes.punchline: Even her virtual family life is dysfunctional
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Liz self-deprecation - even her escapist video game life is tragic, with dark implication about murder

jokes.text: Like Hilary... From Fresh Prince of Bel-Air.
jokes.setup: Tracy calls Liz amazing, strong, and intelligent woman like Hillary...
jokes.punchline: References sitcom character instead of Hillary Clinton
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Tracy's pop culture-focused worldview over political figures

jokes.text: 'these are all hookers. Pick one.'
jokes.setup: Jack remembers first thing Don Geiss said to him
jokes.punchline: Casual instruction to choose from group of prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate culture satire with casual treatment of corruption as mentorship

jokes.text: That realization led him to develop the nightlight And the marketing campaign aimed at making children Afraid of the dark-- a monster under every bed.
jokes.setup: Jack explains Don Geiss's insight about untapped market of sleeping people
jokes.punchline: Nightlight success through deliberately terrifying children
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate satire revealing how companies create demand through psychological manipulation of children

jokes.text: Three words: Porn for women.
jokes.setup: Jack promises revolutionary business idea for untapped market
jokes.punchline: His innovation is 'porn for women' but completely misunderstands what that would be
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - thinks he understands women while completely missing the point

jokes.text: To jabber.
jokes.setup: Jack says women have one insatiable need
jokes.punchline: Reduces all female communication to mindless 'jabbering'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack misogyny delivered with corporate confidence, perfect character integration

jokes.text: And I'm ready to make.
jokes.setup: Jack's speech about American capitalism and consumption
jokes.punchline: Simple declaration of intent to return to productivity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's renewed sense of purpose. Simple but effective conclusion to his arc.

jokes.text: That is insane! No, it's insane That the actor Wesley Snipes has that name.
jokes.setup: Liz shocked that Wesley's name is Wesley Snipes
jokes.punchline: Wesley thinks the actor stole his more appropriate name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Wesley's delusional self-image and racial obliviousness

jokes.text: That's what we call spring cleaning in England.
jokes.setup: Wesley mentions sweeps
jokes.punchline: Claims 'sweeps' is British term for spring cleaning
jokes.explanation: Standard Wesley pretentious explanation, but less clever than his better moments

jokes.text: 'uh-huh,' 'how annoying,' 'she's clearly jealous of you,' And 'well, it's his loss.'
jokes.setup: Jack's 'porn for women' concept - men who listen patiently
jokes.punchline: Lists generic supportive responses men are programmed to give
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of Jack's concept with accurate catalog of supportive responses

tmdb_overview: Jack needs to reestablish himself within the new corporate structure. Liz and Wesley continually run into each other, making them consider fate's role in their lives. Tracy's nanny writes an exposé.
insight: Season 4's densest episode delivers 61 jokes in pure character-comedy mode.
